entries.title: Arcane Jaspr
entries.excerpt: Arcane Jaspr Codex Documentation for Arcane Jaspr, a component system for Jaspr with Shadcn/Codex renderer switching. Current Snapshot (March 3, 2026) - Shadcn-aligned functional components - exported component modules total - foundation modules outside the functional cap Use This First - $1 Key Features Arcane Jaspr Codex delivers production-ready documentation primitives: Start with environment requirements and setup flow. Create your first page and wire navigation in minutes. Browse all

entries.title: Introduction
entries.description: Arcane Jaspr documentation and current component catalog snapshot
entries.excerpt: Introduction Arcane Jaspr is a Flutter-like UI layer for Jaspr with semantic HTML output and theme-driven renderers. Current Snapshot (March 3, 2026) - $1 - $1 - $1 Source of Truth Use the component catalog as the canonical reference for what is currently supported: - $1 Start Here - $1 - $1 - $1 - $1 - $1 Documentation Scope This docs site now tracks the current consolidated surface only. The $1 page is the authoritative current-state index.

entries.title: Installation
entries.description: How to add Arcane Jaspr to your project
entries.excerpt: Installation There are two ways to get started with Arcane Jaspr: using Oracular (recommended) or manual setup. Option 1: Using Oracular (Recommended) Oracular is the official scaffolding tool for Arcane projects. It creates production-ready projects with best practices built in. Install Oracular Create a New Project This creates a complete project structure with: - Arcane Jaspr 2.7.0 pre-configured - Theming setup with ArcaneApp - Sample screens and components - Proper routing configuration -

entries.title: Quick Start
entries.description: Get up and running in 5 minutes
entries.excerpt: Quick Start Get your first documentation page live in under 5 minutes. [!WARNING] Java 21 Required Arcane Jaspr tooling requires Java 21 or newer for full compatibility. Creating Your First Page Add a new file under with frontmatter and body content. Set , , and so the page is indexed and themed correctly. Add or update ordering in the folder so the page appears where you expect. Start the dev server, open the page, and confirm TOC, callouts, cards, and page footer nav. Mintlify-style Yes.

entries.title: Why Jaspr?
entries.description: When to choose Jaspr over Flutter for web development
entries.excerpt: Why Jaspr + Arcane Jaspr? This guide helps you understand when Jaspr is the right choice for your web project, especially compared to Flutter web. The Core Difference $1 renders your UI to a canvas element. The browser sees a single tag, not your buttons, text, or links. $1 renders to semantic HTML. Your button is a , your text is a , your link is an . The browser understands your content. This fundamental difference affects everything. --- SEO and Discoverability Search Engine Indexing |

entries.title: Component Aliases
entries.description: Use shorter A* names for all Arcane components
entries.excerpt: Component Aliases Tired of typing , , everywhere? We get it. That's why we created short-hand aliases for every component. Using Aliases Instead of importing the main library: Import the aliases file: The file re-exports everything from the main library, so you don't lose access to anything. You just gain shorter names. The Pattern Every component has an alias: | Full Name | Alias | |-----------|-------| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ...and so on for all 100+

entries.title: Styling
entries.description: Learn how to style components using ArcaneStyleData
entries.excerpt: Styling Arcane Jaspr uses for declarative, type-safe styling. This approach provides autocomplete, compile-time checking, and consistent design tokens. ArcaneStyleData The class is the foundation of all styling in Arcane Jaspr. It converts typed properties into CSS. Basic Usage Using Raw CSS For properties not covered by typed enums, use the parameter: Static Presets ArcaneStyleData provides static presets for common layouts: Row Layout Row with Space Between Column Layout Centered Content Full

entries.title: Theming
entries.description: Learn how to configure stylesheets and theming in Arcane Jaspr
entries.excerpt: Theming Arcane Jaspr uses a stylesheet-based theming system built on CSS custom properties. Stylesheets provide complete design systems including colors, typography, spacing, and component renderers. ArcaneApp Every Arcane application must be wrapped in an component which provides the theme context and injects the stylesheet CSS. Available Stylesheets Arcane Jaspr includes two stylesheets: | Stylesheet | Description | |------------|-------------| | | Minimal, modern, accessible. Based on

entries.title: Design Tokens
entries.description: Reference for all Arcane UI design tokens
entries.excerpt: Design Tokens Arcane Jaspr uses a comprehensive design token system for consistent styling. These tokens are available as both Dart constants and CSS custom properties. Colors (ArcaneColors) All colors are available as CSS variables with the prefix. Brand & Accent Colors | Token | CSS Variable | Description | |-------|--------------|-------------| | | | Primary accent color | | | | Accent hover state | | | | Accent container background | | | | Text on accent background | Status Colors | Token |

entries.title: Borders
entries.description: Reference for border, radius, and outline enums
entries.excerpt: Borders These enums control border styling including presets, radius, width, and style. BorderPreset Complete border configurations combining width, style, and color. Basic Borders | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | | No border | | | 1px subtle border | | | 1px standard border | | | 1px medium visibility | | | 1px light border | Semantic Borders | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | | 1px accent color | | | 1px success color | | | 1px warning color | | | 1px error

entries.title: Colors
entries.description: Reference for background, text, and border color enums
entries.excerpt: Colors These enums provide semantic color values that adapt to the current theme. Background Background color presets. Core Backgrounds | Value | CSS Variable | Description | |-------|-------------|-------------| | | | Transparent background | | | | Page background | | | | Secondary background | | | | Tertiary background | Surface & Card | Value | CSS Variable | Description | |-------|-------------|-------------| | | | Surface background | | | | Alternate surface | | | | Card background | | | |

entries.title: Display & Layout
entries.description: Reference for display, position, and flex layout enums
entries.excerpt: Display & Layout These enums control the display mode, positioning, and flex/grid layout of elements. Display Controls how an element is displayed. | Value | CSS Output | Description | |-------|-----------|-------------| | | | Hidden, removed from layout | | | | Block-level element | | | | Inline element | | | | Inline with block behavior | | | | Flex container | | | | Inline flex container | | | | Grid container | | | | Inline grid container | | | | Only children are rendered | | | | Block

entries.title: Effects
entries.description: Reference for shadow, transition, transform, and other effect enums
entries.excerpt: Effects These enums control visual effects including shadows, transitions, transforms, and more. Shadow Box shadow presets. Basic Shadows | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | | No shadow | | | Extra small shadow | | | Small shadow | | | Medium shadow | | | Large shadow | | | Extra large shadow | | | Card-specific shadow | Glow Effects | Value | Description | |-------|-------------| | | Accent color glow | | | Success color glow | | | Error color glow | | | Primary color glow | | |

entries.title: Spacing
entries.description: Reference for padding, margin, and gap enums
entries.excerpt: Spacing These enums provide consistent spacing throughout your application. Spacing Base spacing scale used by other spacing enums. | Value | CSS Output | Use Case | |-------|-----------|----------| | | | No spacing | | | | Tight spacing | | | | Small spacing | | | | Default spacing | | | | Large spacing | | | | Extra large | | | | Section spacing | | | | Large sections | | | | Hero sections | | | | Full sections | Properties PaddingPreset Preset padding configurations. Uniform Padding | Value

entries.title: Typography
entries.description: Reference for font size, weight, family, and text styling enums
entries.excerpt: Typography These enums control text appearance including size, weight, family, and decoration. FontSize Font size scale from extra small to mega display. | Value | CSS Output | Use Case | |-------|-----------|----------| | | | Small labels, captions | | | | Secondary text, metadata | | | | Default body text | | | | Standard body text | | | | Emphasized text | | | | Subheadings | | | | Section headings | | | | Page headings | | | | Large headings | | | | Display headings | | | | Hero text | | |

entries.title: Deployment
entries.excerpt: Deployment Deploy your static documentation site to various hosting providers. Firebase Hosting The template includes Firebase Hosting configuration. Setup 1. Install Firebase CLI: 2. Login to Firebase: 3. Initialize (already done in template): Deploy Build and deploy in one command: Or use the script: Other Providers Since the output is static HTML, you can deploy anywhere: Vercel Netlify GitHub Pages 1. Build the site 2. Copy contents to your branch 3. Enable GitHub Pages in repository
